description: A heap use-after-free vulnerability in V8's d8 shell dynamic module import handling. When a dynamic import is performed (e.g., `import('...')`), `Shell::HostImportModuleWithPhaseDynamically` allocates a `DynamicImportData` object on the native heap and enqueues a `CallbackTask` microtask via `isolate->EnqueueMicrotask(Shell::DoHostImportModuleDynamically, data)`. The `CallbackTask` stores the callback function pointer and the `DynamicImportData*` pointer as raw `Foreign` objects (tagged pointers inside the V8 sandbox). Because these Foreign values are stored in V8-heap-allocated objects, an attacker with sandbox write primitives can overwrite the `data` Foreign pointer of one `CallbackTask` to match that of another, causing two microtasks to share the same `DynamicImportData*`. When the first microtask executes `DoHostImportModuleDynamically`, it reads fields from the `DynamicImportData` and then frees it via `data->DeleteDynamicImportData(import_data_)`. When the second microtask runs with the now-freed pointer, it accesses freed memory at `import_data_->isolate`, triggering a heap-use-after-free. The PoC confirms this by using `Sandbox.MemoryView` to overwrite the data Foreign value in one CallbackTask, then triggering both microtasks to execute.

fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6917687 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
